claim_status: Fresh-host readiness-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no release-operations review, no quality review, no independent fresh-host reproduction, no owner-approved clean-VM equivalent, no command execution review, no retained bootstrap/doctor/check/xtask logs, no exact host-fact review, no source identity review, no cache or target-directory review, no source-tree cleanliness review, no failure-denominator review, no rollback or cleanup review, no environmental-waiver review, no PB-009 readiness promotion, no broad M1 readiness, no preview readiness, no beta readiness, no stable readiness, no production readiness, no release confidence, no Chrome-class claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not leave null fields in a real review or name placeholder run records, reference hosts, clean-VM waivers, owner reviewers, independent reviewers, release-operations reviewers, quality reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, same-host evidence, or implied PB-009 readiness authority.

host_identity_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed run record proves the host is an independent fresh reference host beyond the checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template.
If a clean VM is used, owner-reviewed waiver evidence records owner, reason, scope, expiry, isolation, and cleanup beyond the checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template.
Exact operating system edition, version, architecture, patch level, locale, timezone, and shell are retained with the run record.
CPU, memory, disk, virtualization state, power posture, privilege level, and network posture are recorded before commands run.
Evidence proves no prior Turing target directories, Cargo build outputs, local profiles, downloaded installers, or generated artifacts are reused.
Environment snapshot records PATH, CARGO_TARGET_DIR, Cargo cache root, Rustup toolchain cache, temp directory, and redacted sensitive values.
Privilege level, execution policy, network policy, and any security-tool interference are retained and reviewed.
Cleanup evidence proves temporary host state, caches, logs, and partial build output are retained or removed according to the reviewed policy.

source_checkout_axes.required_evidence: Remote URL, branch, commit hash, checkout command, and Git version are retained before bootstrap runs.
Git line-ending configuration and repository line-ending policy are captured so fresh-host output can be compared honestly.
Source-tree status is captured before bootstrap and rejects pre-existing generated output, untracked installers, archives, credentials, local profiles, or dirty files.
Source-tree status is captured after bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask commands and classifies every changed, ignored, generated, or untracked path.
Owner review confirms the run record corresponds to the intended source commit and not to local transfer, patched checkout, stale branch, or hidden build output.
Generated build output is kept outside durable source or covered by explicit ignore and cleanup evidence.
Source checkout evidence proves no credentials, private profiles, secrets, downloaded archives, or installers are committed or retained in the repository.
Rollback baseline records which paths can be reverted and which generated caches are outside source before any readiness promotion.

command_execution_axes.required_evidence: Retained bootstrap output from `sh tools/bootstrap.sh` or `.\tools\bootstrap.ps1` includes command line, shell, timestamps, exit code, stdout/stderr path, SHA-256, and failure classification.
Retained doctor output from `sh tools/doctor.sh` or `.\tools\doctor.ps1` includes command line, shell, timestamps, Rust, Cargo, Git, exit code, stdout/stderr path, SHA-256, and read-only behavior.

Retained check output from `sh tools/check.sh` or `.\tools\check.ps1` includes aggregate validation, cargo fmt, clippy, workspace tests, shell self-test, prototype output, exit code, stdout/stderr path, and SHA-256.
Retained `cargo run --locked -p xtask -- bootstrap` output proves wrapper equivalence or records owner-approved wrapper-equivalent reason.
Retained `cargo run --locked -p xtask -- doctor --ci` output proves wrapper equivalence or records owner-approved wrapper-equivalent reason.
Retained `cargo run --locked -p xtask -- check` output proves wrapper equivalence or records owner-approved wrapper-equivalent reason.
Every attempted command, including failures, timeouts, cancellations, and reruns, is retained in the denominator before owner review.
Same-host reruns are labeled current-host diagnostics and cannot support independent reproducibility, PB-009 readiness, release confidence, production readiness, or Chrome-class claims.

cache_artifact_axes.required_evidence: CARGO_TARGET_DIR and actual target directory are recorded before and after commands, with proof that build output stays outside durable source when required.
Cargo cache root and reused registry state are recorded, classified, and covered by owner-approved cache policy or clean-host proof.
Rustup toolchain cache state is recorded, including installed toolchain, components, and whether it was preexisting or installed during bootstrap.
Temporary directories, downloaded archives, generated packages, and retained logs are bounded, hashed where retained, and cleaned up or intentionally preserved.
Retained bootstrap, doctor, check, xtask, source-tree status, and environment logs include SHA-256 hashes and storage locations.
Retention policy states which logs, failure artifacts, cache facts, and cleanup records are kept for review without committing generated junk.

failure_review_axes.required_evidence: Failures are classified as environmental, source-controlled, network-dependent, nondeterministic, timeout, cancellation, disk-full, permission, cache, or toolchain issues.
Failed runs remain in the denominator until owner review accepts a rerun policy and documents the reason.
Rollback notes explain cleanup for partial installs, target directories, generated output, cache changes, and source-tree changes.
Environmental waivers name owner, reason, scope, expiry, reference host impact, cleanup evidence, and whether PB-009 can still be promoted.
Network-dependent bootstrap or package-fetch behavior is retained and separated from source-controlled build failures.

Owner review states whether the run supports only contained M0 reproduction or can promote PB-009, and records remaining preview, beta, stable, production, release-confidence, and Chrome-class boundaries.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Owner accepts the run record, reference host, source identity, command output, failure denominator, waivers, and PB-009 status change beyond the checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template.
Independent reviewer verifies retained logs, hashes, source-tree status, clean-host claim, cache behavior, and failure classification.
Release-operations reviewer accepts bootstrap, doctor, check, xtask, target-directory, cache, packaging, and release-confidence implications.
Quality reviewer accepts command denominator, rerun policy, failure classification, cleanup evidence, and validation output.
Security-sensitive host, credential, download, installer, profile, and log-retention concerns are reviewed before production or release claims.
Pre-build readiness, task queue, research crosswalk, operating board, repository map, and research index are updated after review.
Owner approval records expiry, host scope, source commit, toolchain scope, and whether a stale run must be repeated.
PB-009 readiness promotion remains separate from broad M1, preview, beta, stable, production, release-confidence, implementation, and Chrome-class approval.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template is cited as independent fresh-host reproduction or PB-009 readiness evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject PB-009 readiness, independent reproducibility, release confidence, production readiness, implementation, and Chrome-class claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Run record, reference host, clean-VM waiver, owner reviewer, independent reviewer, release-operations reviewer, or quality reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Same-host reruns or current-host diagnostics are cited as independent fresh-host or owner-approved clean-VM evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject independent reproducibility and PB-009 promotion claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Bootstrap, doctor, check, or xtask command output lacks command line, shell, timestamps, exit code, stdout/stderr retention path, SHA-256, or failure evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject command execution review and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Operating system, shell, locale, timezone, CPU, memory, disk, virtualization, network posture, privilege, or prior-state evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject fresh-host identity and clean-VM equivalence claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Remote URL, branch, commit hash, Git version, line-ending policy, or source-tree status is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject source identity and reproducibility claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Changed, untracked, ignored, generated, installer, archive, credential, local profile, or temporary paths are hidden or left unexplained.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject source-tree cleanliness and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: CARGO_TARGET_DIR, target directory, Cargo cache root, Rustup cache, temp directory, downloaded archive, or retained-artifact behavior is missing or ambiguous.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject cache, target-directory, and clean-host claims.

rejection_rules.condition: The run record omits failed commands, timeouts, cancellations, disk-full, permission, network, cache, toolchain, or nondeterministic failures.
A clean-VM or environmental waiver lacks owner, reason, scope, expiry, cleanup evidence, or claim impact.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner-approved clean-VM equivalence and PB-009 promotion claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing validation on one host is cited as preview, beta, stable, release-confidence, production, implementation, daily-driver, or Chrome-class evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject release, production, implementation, and Chrome-class claims.

rejection_rules.condition: A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, same-host rejection, no-claim wording, claim boundary wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement owner-reviewed evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-009 partial.
